title: Driver for a memory-in-pixel display
abstract: A memory cell for a display is disclosed. The memory cell has a current limiter on the power supply to reduce the power consumed by the memory cell during a write operation. The level of the current limiter is set for each pixel by a driver so that the current drawn by the memory cell is reduced but still large enough to change the state of the memory cell. The driver can maintain this balance despite changing conditions.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A display comprising: a pixel including: a memory cell; and a current-limiting transistor coupled between the memory cell and a power supply; and a driver coupled at an output a bias voltage to a controlling terminal of the current-limiting transistor, the driver including: a reference current source configured to generate a reference current at a limited level, the reference cunent generating the bias voltage relative to the power supply so that the current-limiting transistor conducts a current at the limited level independent of the power supply. 2. The display according to claim 1, further including a reference voltage source coupled between the power supply and a reference node of the driver, the reference voltage source configured to generate a reference voltage based on the reference current. 3. The display according to claim 2, wherein the reference voltage source includes: a bias-control transistor configured to generate a first voltage based on the reference current, wherein the first voltage approximately equal to a threshold voltage of the currentlimiting transistor; and an overdrive transistor configured to generate a second voltage based on the reference current, wherein the second voltage is approximately equal to an overdrive voltage of the current-limiting transistor. 4. The display according to claim 3, wherein: the bias-control transistor is configured as a first diode; and the overdrive transistor is configured as a second diode. 5. The display according to claim 3 or 4, wherein the reference node is at the bias voltage, the bias voltage being the power supply minus the reference voltage. 6. The display according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein: 

 the current-limiting transistor is a p-type MOSFET; the cunent-limiting transistor is coupled at a source terminal to the power supply; the cunent-limiting transistor is coupled at a gate terminal to the driver; and the cunent-limiting transistor is coupled at a drain terminal to the memory cell. 7. The display according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein: the memory cell includes a pair of inverters coupled output to input; the cunentlimiting transistor coupled to between the power supply of the pair of inverters. 8. The display according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the limited level is: below a shoot-through current level conducted by the memory cell during a write operation of the memory cell without the cunent-limiting transistor; and above a minimum level required to change a state of the memory cell. 9. The display according to claim 8, wherein: the display includes a plurality of pixels: the driver is coupled to a plurality of cunent-limiting transistors of the plurality of pixels; and the limited level is above the minimum level by a margin corresponding to a variation in a plurality of memory cells of the plurality of pixels. 10. The display according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the driver further includes: a reference voltage source coupled between the power supply and a reference node of the driver, the reference voltage source configured to generate a reference voltage based on the reference current; and a voltage mirror circuit configured to mirror the bias voltage at the reference node to at the output, the bias voltage being the power supply minus the reference voltage. 11. The display according to claim 10, wherein the voltage mirror circuit includes: a first transistor coupled between the reference node and the reference current source, the first transistor configured as a diode; a second transistor coupled between the output and a ground, the second transistor and the first transistor being coupled together at respective gate terminals; and 

 a second current source coupled between the power supply and the output. 12. The display according to claim 1 1, wherein: the second transistor includes a plurality of transistors coupled in a parallel connection with the second transistor, the plurality of transistors substantially matching the second transistor; and the second current source includes a plurality of current sources coupled in parallel with the second current source, the plurality of current sources substantially matching the reference current source, wherein the plurality of transistors and the plurality 7 of current sources are configured to generate a bias current at the output of the driver sufficient to drive a plurality of current-limiting transistors. 13. The display according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the pixel further includes: a micro-LED coupled to a current source by a switch, the switch controlled ON/OFF according to a state of the memory cell. 14. A method for controlling current to a memory 7 cell of a pixel, the method comprising: coupling a bias-control transistor and an overdrive transistor between a power supply and a reference node of a driver controlling a current-limiting transistor; generating a bias voltage at the reference node based on a reference voltage across the bias-control transistor and the overdrive transistor; mirroring the bias voltage to an output of the driver; coupling the bias voltage to a gate terminal of the current-limiting transistor while the memory cell is in a write mode; and configuring the current-limiting transistor to control the current to the memory cell to a limited level based on a voltage, which is not affected by changes to the power supply. 15. The method according to claim 14, wherein: the bias-control transistor is configured as a first diode to generate a first voltage, the first voltage approximately equal to a threshold voltage of the current-limiting transistor; and the overdrive transistor is configured as a second diode to generate a second voltage, the second voltage approximately equal to an overdrive voltage of the current-limiting transistor. 16. The method according to claim 14 or 15, further comprising: coupling the power supply to the gate terminal of the current-limiting transistor while the memory cell is not in the write mode. 17. The method according to any one of claims 14 to 16, wherein the level is below a shoot-through current level conducted by the memory cell during a write operation of the memory cell without the current-limiting transistor. 18. The method according to claim 17, further including configuring the memory cell to change a state based on the current conducted to the memory cell by the current-limiting transistor. 19. A display comprising: a pluralityâ of memory cells configured to draw current through current-limiting transistors, the current-limiting transistors each coupled between a respective memory cell and a power supply; and a driver configured to transmit a bias voltage to gate terminals of the current-limiting transistors during a write period, the bias voltage configuring the current-limiting transistors to operate in a partially-ON condition to limit the cunent drawn by the plurality of memory cells, wherein the bias voltage is relative to the powder supply to compensate for variations in the powder supply. 20. The display according to claim 19. wherein the plurality of memory cells are six- transistor static random access memory (6T-SRAM) cells.
